# Generated by ollama (codegemma)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for our complete application stack (VPC, EC2, RDS, S3, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure components. We'll add CloudWatch alarms and detailed monitoring after we deploy and verify the basic setup works.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

Resources:
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 192.168.0.0/16

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 192.168.1.0/24

  PrivateSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 192.168.2.0/24

  SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      GroupDescription: Allow inbound traffic on port 80

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t2.micro
      ImageId: ami-0a8444b4a88564b4a # Replace with your desired AMI ID
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref SecurityGroup

  RDSDatabase:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    Properties:
      Engine: MySQL
      EngineVersion: 8.0.28
      AllocatedStorage: 20
      DBInstanceClass: db.t2.micro
      DBName: mydatabase

  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: mybucket

  LoadBalancer:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancing::LoadBalancer
    Properties:
      Subnets:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref SecurityGroup
      Listeners:
        - Protocol: HTTP
          Port: 80
          InstancePort: 80
          LoadBalancerPort: 80
